Title: Elucidation of Spartina dimethylsulfoniopropionate synthesis genes enables engineering of stress tolerant plants

Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: pOsUBI3 (constitutive, used for SaMMT in engineering); co-expression with p35S-SaSDC and pAtUBI10-SaDOX in transient/stable engineering
Common_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Spartina anglica; Nicotiana benthamiana; Arabidopsis thaliana
Common_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: A. thaliana Col-0; tomato cv. Micro Tom used for exogenous DMSP assays, not for SaMMT transgenics
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful as one component of a three-gene DMSP engineering stack with SaSDC and SaDOX; SaMMT alone was insufficient to raise DMSP strongly, but combined expression enabled high DMSP accumulation and improved salt/drought tolerance. Deploy with moderate expression and trait testing because excessive DMSP production may incur metabolic cost.
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supplies the first methylation step of the plant DMSP pathway and contributes to elevated DMSP accumulation only when combined with SaSDC and SaDOX.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transcriptome mining; recombinant protein purification and in vitro MMT enzyme assay; tissue RT-qPCR; transient co-expression in N. benthamiana; stable overexpression with SaSDC and SaDOX in A. thaliana; DMSP metabolite profiling by GC/LC-MS.
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Natural saltmarsh habitat; sulfate-rich sea-salt treatment increased DMSP accumulation, whereas NaCl alone did not; engineering tested under salt and drought stress.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: In S. anglica, transcript detected across tissues and higher in leaves than other tissues, but less striking than SaSDC.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: In S. anglica, higher in leaves.
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: S. anglica RNA-seq transcriptome; differential-expression RNA-seq for high- vs low-DMSP clumps; NCBI SRA BioSample accessions SAMN42957141; SAMN42997936
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: MMT activity itself was not a determinant of high-level DMSP production across species; homologous plant MMTs from low-DMSP species had comparable in vitro activity.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: High DMSP-producing transgenic lines suggested possible metabolic cost or loss of protection above an optimal accumulation threshold, though this was not assigned specifically to SaMMT alone.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SaMMT1 is an active Spartina methionine S-methyltransferase catalyzing the first step toward DMSP synthesis. 2. Its expression/activity pattern supports participation in leaf DMSP production but it is not by itself the key determinant of Spartina's exceptionally high DMSP. 3. In combination with SaSDC and SaDOX, SaMMT1 enables engineering of high DMSP accumulation and abiotic stress tolerance in heterologous plants.
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Predominantly leaves in S. anglica; engineered accumulation in leaves of N. benthamiana and A. thaliana
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Osmotic and oxidative stress protection; contributes to drought and salinity tolerance; precursor of climate-active gases DMS and methanethiol

Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Not recommended as an engineering target for DMSP enhancement because it lacked detectable MMT activity and was concluded not to contribute to SMM cycling or DMSP production.
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: No detectable MMT activity; not involved in SMM cycling or DMSP production.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Sequence comparison revealing 10-aa insertion in conserved SAM-binding domain; recombinant in vitro enzyme assay showing no MMT activity.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Identified as a transcript in S. anglica leaf transcriptome.
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: S. anglica leaf RNA-seq transcriptome; NCBI SRA BioSample accessions SAMN42957141; SAMN42997936
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Contains a 10 amino acid insertion in the conserved S-adenosylmethionine-binding domain, explaining loss of activity.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SaMMT2 was identified as an MMT-like transcript from S. anglica. 2. A conserved-domain insertion abolished detectable MMT activity. 3. The paper concludes SaMMT2 does not contribute to DMSP synthesis.
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Leaves are the main synthesis site in S. anglica, but SaMMT2 itself is not functional in the pathway.
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Osmotic and oxidative stress protection; precursor of climate-active sulfur gases

Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Secondary/uncertain candidate rather than preferred target. It possesses low DOX activity, but SaDOX is the superior engineering gene because of much higher catalytic activity and stronger evidence for pathway relevance.
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Shows DOX activity toward DMSP-amine, but is ~45-fold less active than SaDOX and its contribution to whole-plant non-leaf DOX activity remains unresolved.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Recombinant protein assay for DOX activity; mass spectrometry identification in active chromatographic fractions; tissue activity comparisons.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transcript detected in S. anglica; tissue-specific pattern less emphasized than SaSDC/SaDOX.
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: S. anglica RNA-seq transcriptome; LC-MS/MS of active protein fractions; NCBI SRA BioSample accessions SAMN42957141; SAMN42997936
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Unlike SaDOX, SaCAO1 showed no activity toward putrescine. The authors note non-leaf DOX activity could derive from SaCAO1, SaDOX and/or unknown enzymes.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Lower catalytic efficiency makes it less attractive for engineering high DMSP flux.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SaCAO1 is a Spartina copper amine oxidase with measurable DOX activity. 2. Its activity is much lower than SaDOX, so it is unlikely to be the principal determinant of high DMSP synthesis. 3. It may contribute to residual/non-leaf DOX activity, but this remains unresolved.
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Leaves are the main synthesis site in S. anglica, though DMSP is also present in other tissues.
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Osmotic and oxidative stress protection; precursor of DMS and methanethiol

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: p35S (constitutive, used for SaSDC in engineering); co-expression with pOsUBI3-SaMMT and pAtUBI10-SaDOX
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Spartina anglica; Nicotiana benthamiana; Arabidopsis thaliana
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Methionine methylation pathway for DMSP biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Highest-priority engineering target for reconstituting plant DMSP synthesis because the paper identifies SDC as the most critical enzyme enabling high-level DMSP production in Spartina. Best deployed in a three-gene cassette with SaMMT1 and SaDOX; consider tuning expression because very high DMSP may reduce benefit or add metabolic cost.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: A unique Spartina enzyme activity that is essential for high-level DMSP synthesis; its presence and strong leaf-restricted expression explain major DMSP accumulation in S. anglica.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transcriptome candidate screening; recombinant enzyme assays with SMM, ornithine, arginine and diaminopimelate; kinetic analysis by HPLC/NMR; tissue RT-qPCR and enzyme activity assays; cross-species homolog testing; transient and stable overexpression with companion pathway genes followed by DMSP quantification and stress assays.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms an obligate intermediate (DMSP-amine) en route to DMSP; effectively a pathway-defining step in Spartina
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Leaf tissue is the major synthesis site; DMSP accumulation in the plant is associated with abiotic stress and sulfate availability rather than NaCl alone.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Very strongly enriched in S. anglica leaves; transcripts and corresponding SDC activity were virtually undetectable outside leaves.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: S. anglica transcriptome RNA-seq; high- vs low-DMSP clump RNA-seq; NCBI SRA BioSample accessions SAMN42957141; SAMN42997936
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Originally identified among decarboxylase candidates as an ornithine decarboxylase-like protein (SaODC), but it showed virtually no ornithine activity and clear SMM decarboxylase activity. Homologous ODCs from S. officinarum, S. patens, S. viridis and S. lycopersicum lacked SDC activity, indicating convergent evolution of high DMSP production and making SaSDC the distinguishing biochemical innovation in Spartina.
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Central DMSP biosynthetic branch from methionine via SMM
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Very high DMSP-producing transgenic plants may experience metabolic cost or diminishing protective return at extreme accumulation levels.
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: PLP-dependent amino acid decarboxylase; ornithine decarboxylase-like enzyme
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as the most critical/key control innovation for high-level DMSP synthesis in Spartina
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SaSDC catalyzes conversion of SMM to DMSP-amine and is the first plant SDC identified. 2. It is highly leaf-specific in expression/activity and is the strongest determinant of Spartina's exceptional DMSP production. 3. Homologs from other species lacked SDC activity, showing that evolution of this altered decarboxylase function underlies convergent high-DMSP evolution. 4. Together with SaMMT1 and SaDOX, SaSDC enables DMSP engineering and stress tolerance in heterologous plants.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Synthesized mainly in leaves of S. anglica and then likely mobilized to other tissues; engineered accumulation in N. benthamiana and A. thaliana leaves
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Abiotic stress protection against osmotic and oxidative stress; promotes salinity and drought tolerance; precursor of climate-active sulfur gases

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: pAtUBI10 (constitutive, used for SaDOX in engineering); co-expression with pOsUBI3-SaMMT and p35S-SaSDC
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Spartina anglica; Nicotiana benthamiana; Arabidopsis thaliana
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Methionine methylation pathway for DMSP biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Important engineering component because high DOX expression is needed for strong DMSP accumulation in heterologous plants. Best used in combination with SaMMT1 and SaSDC; expression level appears important for pathway flux.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxidatively deaminates DMSP-amine to DMSP-aldehyde, which is then converted to DMSP by endogenous/promiscuous aldehyde dehydrogenase activity.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Major DOX enzyme in S. anglica; high catalytic activity and especially elevated expression contribute strongly to high whole-plant DOX activity and DMSP accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transcriptome mining; recombinant enzyme assays and approximate kinetic estimation; activity-guided protein fractionation with mass spectrometry; tissue RT-qPCR/activity assays; cross-species homolog assays; transient and stable overexpression with SaMMT1 and SaSDC followed by DMSP/stress phenotyping.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms the immediate precursor of DMSP and drives flux toward terminal product
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Leaf tissue has highest DOX activity; DMSP accumulation overall is linked to abiotic stress and sulfate-rich conditions.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transcript present in S. anglica, with leaf-enriched pathway expression; across species, DOX transcripts are 33-84-fold more abundant in S. anglica than in low-DMSP producers.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Elevated in S. anglica relative to low-producing plant homologs; associated with leaf synthesis tissues.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: S. anglica RNA-seq transcriptome; comparative transcript abundance analyses across species; LC-MS/MS identification in active fractions; NCBI SRA BioSample accessions SAMN42957141; SAMN42997936
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Identified initially as SaCAO2, a copper amine oxidase. Unlike the main catalytic divergence seen for SaSDC, DOX activity is fairly widespread in plants; the crucial Spartina difference is much higher expression, not radically unique enzymology. SaDOX also accepts putrescine and is strongly inhibited by excess putrescine.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Possible competition with polyamine metabolism or diminishing benefit at extreme DMSP accumulation; excessive total DMSP in transgenics may reduce shoot-growth benefit.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control via elevated expression in S. anglica; not uniquely rate-limiting by enzyme chemistry alone
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SaDOX is the principal Spartina DMSP-amine oxidase, far more active than SaCAO1. 2. DOX-like activity is common in plants, but SaDOX is expressed much more strongly in S. anglica, generating much higher total pathway flux. 3. Together with SaMMT1 and SaSDC, SaDOX is required for engineered high DMSP accumulation and abiotic stress tolerance.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Main synthesis in S. anglica leaves with DMSP distributed to other tissues; engineered accumulation in leaves of N. benthamiana and A. thaliana
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Osmotic and oxidative stress protection; salinity and drought tolerance; precursor to DMS and methanethiol
